title: AI Authoring Request v1
description: Typed request for a single AI-generated changeset, which must be returned as a prism envelope (ai-authoring-response-v1).

requestId.description: Stable identifier for this authoring request.

clientId.description: ID of the client or service making this request.

repo.description: Target repository for the generated artifacts (e.g., org/repo).

branch.description: Preferred target branch; tools may map this to a feature branch.

tier.description: Governance tier for this authoring operation (1=public, 2=vault, 3=lab).

preferredPaths.description: Optional hints for where generated artifacts should be placed.

summary.description: Short description of what the caller wants the AI to do.

details.description: Optional freeform natural-language instructions for the AI.

maxArtifacts.description: Maximum number of artifacts the AI may include in the prism envelope.

noRawContent.description: If true, the AI must not generate any raw horror content; contract-only artifacts only.

allowedSchemas.description: Optional whitelist of schemaref URIs the AI may target for artifacts.

allowedRoles.description: Optional whitelist of artifact roles (primary, registry, secondary) the AI may use.

context.description: Optional machine-readable context the AI can use.

referencedIds.description: IDs of regions, personas, styles, or events this request should anchor to.

registryHints.description: Optional hints about which registry files are in play (e.g., core/registry/styles.ndjson).

invariantSnapshot.description: Optional numeric snapshot of invariants (CIC, AOS, etc.) relevant to this request.

metricSnapshot.description: Optional numeric snapshot of entertainment metrics (UEC, EMD, STCI, CDL, ARR).

expectedResponse.description: Explicit contract for the response type.

schemaref.description: The AI must respond with a document that validates against this schema.
